✦ Poem ✦

Old Letters

22 May 2024

I found your letters in a box, tied neatly, brown with age, the ink now pale as winter light on every yellowed page.

You wrote of ordinary things — the weather, bread, the rain — yet every ordinary word contained a world of pain.

How strange that paper holds the dead, their voices, dry and thin, still calling through the years to us who never wrote them in.

✦ ❦ ✦
